assumptions: Worker can regenerate or re-export workbook content without adding external dependencies to the grader environment.
Providing CSVs and a formulas sheet is acceptable as an alternate parseable deliverable.

extra_model_fields.notes_for_worker: Focus first on producing a formula-revealing export and a single-line explicit recommended action in the memo. These two changes address the primary verification blockers and will likely raise semantic credit to full score upon re-evaluation.

assumptions: Worker can regenerate or re-export workbook content without adding external dependencies to the grader environment.
Providing CSVs and a formulas sheet is acceptable as an alternate parseable deliverable.

extra_model_fields.notes_for_worker: Focus first on producing a formula-revealing export and a single-line explicit recommended action in the memo. These two changes address the primary verification blockers and will likely raise semantic credit to full score upon re-evaluation.

planned_changes.description: Regenerate or export workbook deliverable into parser-friendly artifacts: (a) produce a standard .xlsx ensuring formulas are intact, and (b) export key calculation tables as CSV with an adjacent column showing the formula text (or provide a 'Formulas' worksheet listing cell addresses, displayed formulas, and dependencies).
planned_changes.outcome_expected: Graders can open and parse derived values and formulas without depending on environment-specific libraries.

planned_changes.description: Extend the incident_command_memo.md to include a concise Decision Table that lists each scenario, the computed trigger metrics (with worksheet and cell references), the threshold test result, and the explicit recommended action (single line: ACTIVATE / DO NOT ACTIVATE / CONSIDER DIVERSION).
planned_changes.outcome_expected: A clear, easily verifiable decision mapping is present in the memo.

planned_changes.description: Attach a 'calculation_trace.csv' that enumerates each reported metric in the memo, the formula or calculation used, and the exact sheet/cell or named range where it is calculated.
planned_changes.outcome_expected: Independent verification of memo numbers against the workbook will be possible.

planned_changes.description: Include full sensitivity analysis output tables as CSVs and reference them in the memo; ensure memo length is complete and not truncated before submission.
planned_changes.outcome_expected: Sensitivity checks and threshold sweeps will be visible and verifiable.

planned_changes.description: Add a Traceability README or worksheet documenting assumptions, rounding, and how to reproduce each calculation; include a short note for graders about alternate file locations if environment libraries are missing.
planned_changes.outcome_expected: Reduced grader friction and fewer environment-dependent verification failures.

raw_llm_output.revision_reason: Improve artifact parseability and memo clarity so graders can verify workbook formulas, cell-level provenance, sensitivity outputs, and a clear decision recommendation.

metadata_json.revision_goal: Required artifacts present and many intermediate calculations are visible in the memo preview, but the workbook could not be parsed in the evaluation environment and the memo preview is truncated. As a result workbook-level traceability (cell/formula provenance) and an explicit decision table / final recommended action could not be independently verified. Partial semantic credit (0.75) was assigned by the grader and verified with warnings.
